indication: Daratumumab monotherapy is recommended as an option for treating relapsed and refractory multiple myeloma in adults whose previous therapy included a proteasome inhibitor and an immunomodulator ('imid) as a fourth line of treatment, that is, after 3 previous treatments.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Weekly cycles 1-2; fortnightly (days 1 and 15) cycles 3-6; monthly (day 1) cycles 7+
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1-2: weekly (days 1,8,15,22); Cycles 3-6: fortnightly (days 1 and 15); Cycles 7+: monthly (day 1). Infusion rate: First infusion 500ml at 25ml/hr initial rate, increment 25ml/hr per hour, max 100ml/hr. Second infusion 500ml at 50ml/hr initial rate, increment 50ml/hr per hour, max 200ml/hr. Subsequent infusions 500ml at 100ml/hr initial rate, increment 50ml/hr per hour, max 200ml/hr. Rapid infusion protocol (unlicensed): from 3rd infusion onwards in eligible patients – 100ml over 30 minutes then 400ml over 60 minutes (total 90 minutes). Dose banded per national dose bands (20 NS). Prescribed in accordance with national dose bands.

pre_medications.timing: 1-3 hours before every daratumumab infusion
1-3 hours before every daratumumab infusion (cycles 1-2); reduce to 10mg IV/oral from cycle 3 onwards
1-3 hours before every daratumumab infusion
1-3 hours before daratumumab – first two cycles only and first fast infusion if not occurred on cycle 2

take_home_medicines.days: Days 2, 3, 9, 10, 16, 17, 23 and 24 of each cycle
take_home_medicines.indication: Post-infusion steroid to reduce delayed IRR risk

take_home_medicines.days: Days 1-7 of first cycle only
Twice daily for 28 days
Once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday

dose_modifications.condition: Less than 0.5x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia (fever ≥38.5°C and neutrophils <1)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once neutrophils recover to 1x10⁹/L, resume daratumumab at 16mg/kg
Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once platelets recover to 50x10⁹/L or greater resume daratumumab at 16mg/kg
No formal studies conducted. Based on population PK analysis no dosage adjustments are necessary
No dosage adjustment necessary for pre-existing renal impairment

dose_modifications.condition: Mild to moderate infusion related reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Once symptoms resolve, resume infusion at no more than half the rate at which the reaction occurred. Escalate rate incrementally if no further symptoms
If reaction decreases to ≤Grade 2, restart at no more than half the rate at which reaction occurred. Permanently discontinue upon third occurrence of Grade 3 or greater reaction
Permanently discontinue treatment

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle, neutrophil count must be ≥1x10⁹/L and platelet count ≥50x10⁹/L unless low counts due to bone marrow infiltration with myeloma

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
HBV testing before initiating treatment; HCV, CMV and HIV testing if clinically appropriate
Red cell phenotyping required before commencing daratumumab (cross-match fails due to binding to red cells)
Regular blood glucose monitoring
Vital signs before infusion, every 15 minutes during first 60 minutes and at end of infusion
All instances of infusion related reactions must be recorded on ARIA
special_warnings: Daratumumab binds to CD38 on red blood cells causing positive indirect antiglobulin test (Coombs test) – may persist up to 6 months after last infusion; inform blood transfusion
Daratumumab may be detected on serum protein electrophoresis (SPE) and immunofixation (IFE) assays – can give false positive results in IgG kappa myeloma patients
Patients must be given an identification card to carry for 6 months after stopping therapy
First dose must be administered where resuscitation facilities are available; cannula inserted for emergency treatment of anaphylaxis
Rapid infusion protocol (90 minutes) is unlicensed; only for daratumumab monotherapy patients who have tolerated 500ml infusion at manufacturer recommended rates without Grade 1 IRR
No anti-emetics required

drugs.frequency: Weekly cycles 1-2; fortnightly (days 1 and 15) cycles 3-6; monthly (day 1) cycles 7+
drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1-2: weekly (days 1,8,15,22); Cycles 3-6: fortnightly (days 1 and 15); Cycles 7+: monthly (day 1). Infusion rate: First infusion 500ml at 25ml/hr initial rate, increment 25ml/hr per hour, max 100ml/hr. Second infusion 500ml at 50ml/hr initial rate, increment 50ml/hr per hour, max 200ml/hr. Subsequent infusions 500ml at 100ml/hr initial rate, increment 50ml/hr per hour, max 200ml/hr. Rapid infusion protocol (unlicensed): from 3rd infusion onwards in eligible patients – 100ml over 30 minutes then 400ml over 60 minutes (total 90 minutes). Dose banded per national dose bands (20 NS). Prescribed in accordance with national dose bands.
drugs.notes: Weekly cycles 1-2; fortnightly (days 1 and 15) cycles 3-6; monthly (day 1) cycles 7+ | Cycles 1-2: weekly (days 1,8,15,22); Cycles 3-6: fortnightly (days 1 and 15); Cycles 7+: monthly (day 1). Infusion rate: First infusion 500ml at 25ml/hr initial rate, increment 25ml/hr per hour, max 100ml/hr. Second infusion 500ml at 50ml/hr initial rate, increment 50ml/hr per hour, max 200ml/hr. Subsequent infusions 500ml at 100ml/hr initial rate, increment 50ml/hr per hour, max 200ml/hr. Rapid infusion protocol (unlicensed): from 3rd infusion onwards in eligible patients – 100ml over 30 minutes then 400ml over 60 minutes (total 90 minutes). Dose banded per national dose bands (20 NS). Prescribed in accordance with national dose bands.

indication: Pomalidomide, in combination with low-dose dexamethasone, is recommended as an option for treating multiple myeloma in adults at third or subsequent relapse; that is, after 3 previous treatments including both lenalidomide and bortezomib. This protocol with a dose of dexamethasone of 20mg is recommended for those aged over 75 years.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 1mg, 2mg, 3mg and 4mg hard capsules. Swallow whole with water, with or without food. Take at same time each day – advisable to take at night due to drowsiness. If a dose is missed, take the next day at normal dose; do not double up. Press only one end of capsule to remove from blister. All prescriptions must be accompanied by electronic prescription authorisation form (ePAF). Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory.

Starting dose 20mg (dose level 3). Dose level 2: 12mg. Dose level 1: 8mg. Take in morning with or after food. Available as 500microgram, 2mg and 4mg tablets and 2mg/5ml oral liquid. If recovery from toxicities prolonged beyond 14 days, decrease by one dose level.

take_home_medicines.days: Days 1-21 daily
Days 1, 8, 15 and 22
Once daily for 7 days – first cycle only
Twice daily for 28 days
Once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday for 28 days
Daily
take_home_medicines.indication: DVT prophylaxis – choice dependent on risk factors (aspirin 75mg for low risk; LMWH for high risk e.g. dalteparin 5000 units SC OD, enoxaparin 40mg SC OD, heparin 5000 units SC BD)

dose_modifications.notes: First occurrence: interrupt pomalidomide, monitor FBC weekly; once neutrophils recover to 1x10⁹/L resume at 3mg once daily. Each subsequent drop below 0.5x10⁹/L: interrupt and resume at 1mg less than previous dose once neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L

First occurrence: interrupt pomalidomide, monitor FBC weekly; once platelets recover to 50x10⁹/L resume at 3mg once daily. Each subsequent drop: interrupt and resume at 1mg less than previous dose once platelets ≥50x10⁹/L
Patients with serum bilirubin >2mg/dL excluded from clinical studies. No adjustment of starting dose required by Child-Pugh criteria. Monitor LFTs regularly for first 6 months
No dose adjustment required. Where patients receive haemodialysis, pomalidomide dose should be taken following haemodialysis
Reduce pomalidomide dose by 50% if strong inhibitors of CYP1A2 co-administered
Maintain dose and treat with H2 antagonist or PPI. Decrease by one dose level if symptoms persist
Interrupt until symptoms controlled. Add H2 blocker or PPI; decrease one dose level when restarted
Use diuretics as needed; decrease dose by one dose level
Interrupt until symptoms resolve; decrease dose by one level when restarted
Interrupt until muscle weakness is grade 1 or below; restart with dose decreased by one level
Decrease by one dose level. Treat with insulin or oral hypoglycaemic agents as needed
Discontinue patient from dexamethasone treatment regimen permanently
Stop dexamethasone until adverse event resolves to grade 2 or below; resume with dose reduced by one level. If recovery prolonged beyond 14 days, decrease by one dose level

dose_modifications.condition: Venous thrombosis or embolism NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment and start full anticoagulation. May be restarted at clinician's discretion once fully anticoagulated
Interrupt or discontinue pomalidomide. Must be discontinued for angioedema, WHO grade 4 rash, SJS, TEN or DRESS – do not resume
Interrupt during investigation; if ILD confirmed initiate appropriate treatment. Resume only after thorough benefit/risk evaluation

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 adverse reactions related to pomalidomide
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment. Restart when adverse reaction resolves to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below at 1mg less than previous dose, or at consultant's discretion

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle of pomalidomide, neutrophil count must be ≥1x10⁹/L and platelet count must be ≥50x10⁹/L

monitoring_requirements: FBC at baseline then every two weeks for first 8 weeks; thereafter prior to each cycle
U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
Negative pregnancy test within 3 days prior to starting pomalidomide; repeat every 4 weeks (every 2 weeks in women with irregular menstrual cycles); final test 4 weeks after last dose
HBV testing before initiating treatment
VTE risk assessment prior to starting treatment; prescribe thromboprophylaxis
Regular blood glucose monitoring
Cardiac monitoring for signs of cardiac failure and atrial fibrillation
LFT monitoring for first 6 months of treatment then as clinically indicated
special_warnings: Pomalidomide is teratogenic (structurally related to thalidomide); must not be taken during pregnancy. Celgene Pregnancy Prevention Programme (PPP) mandatory for all patients
DVT prophylaxis mandatory: aspirin 75mg once daily for low-risk patients; LMWH for high-risk patients
Cardiac failure occurs in 1/10-1/100 patients; pomalidomide can cause atrial fibrillation precipitating cardiac failure – monitor for signs of cardiac impairment
Interstitial lung disease including pneumonitis has been reported – interrupt if pulmonary symptoms develop
Pomalidomide must only be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist
National Patient Safety Alert NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed
If strong CYP1A2 inhibitors (ciprofloxacin, fluvoxamine) co-administered, reduce pomalidomide dose by 50%
Dexamethasone dose level 3 (starting) = 20mg; level 2 = 12mg; level 1 = 8mg. Protocol for patients aged over 75 years

Press only one end of capsule to remove from blister. All prescriptions must be accompanied by electronic prescription authorisation form (ePAF). Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory.

This protocol with a dose of dexamethasone of 40mg is recommended for those aged 75 years and below.

indication: Daratumumab in combination with lenalidomide and dexamethasone is recommended for use within the Cancer Drugs Fund as an option for treating untreated multiple myeloma patients who are ineligible for an autologous stem cell transplant. Cancer Drug Fund approval – BLUETEQ required prior to treatment. Patient must be contraindicated for thalidomide-containing chemotherapy or intolerant to thalidomide.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Cycles 1-2: weekly (days 1,8,15,22); Cycles 3-6: fortnightly (days 1,15); Cycles 7+: monthly (day 1)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Subcutaneous injection of 15mL solution into abdomen approximately 7.5cm to right or left of umbilicus over approximately 3-5 minutes. Do not inject at other body sites. Rotate injection sites. Never inject into areas where skin is red, bruised, tender, hard or scarred. Do not administer other SC medicines at same site. Pre-medications required 1-3 hours before every daratumumab injection (dexamethasone pre-med serves as the daratumumab pre-med on daratumumab days). First dose: observe with hourly observations for 4 hours. If no IRR following first dose, subsequent doses do not require cannulation or post-injection observations. If IRR with first dose, continue 4-hour observation following subsequent doses until IRRs subside. Regimen based on MAIA trial (SC instead of IV daratumumab; two divided doses of dexamethasone). BLUETEQ approval required.

Take at night (NOCTE) to avoid daytime drowsiness. Start at 25mg and adjust according to toxicity and tolerance. Renal dose adjustments mandatory (see dose modifications). Pregnancy prevention programme (PPP) mandatory. Available as 2.5mg, 5mg, 7.5mg, 10mg, 15mg, 20mg and 25mg capsules. Prescriptions must be accompanied by completed Prescription Authorisation Form.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Cycles 1-2: days 1,2,8,9,15,16,22,23. Cycles 3-6: days 1,2,15,16. Cycles 7+: days 1,2. On daratumumab days the pre-med dexamethasone dose is sufficient; on non-daratumumab days 20mg to be administered
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: 20mg oral (or IV dose equivalent). Reduce to 10mg oral (or IV dose equivalent) for patients over 75 years and BMI less than 18.5. On daratumumab days the pre-med dose of dexamethasone is sufficient. On non-daratumumab days 20mg to be administered.

pre_medications.timing: 1-3 hours prior to every daratumumab injection
pre_medications.indication: IRR prophylaxis – options include chlorphenamine 4mg, loratadine 10mg, cetirizine 10mg, fexofenadine 120mg or acrivastine 8mg oral

pre_medications.timing: Pre-medication on daratumumab days (serves as both pre-med and steroid component)
pre_medications.indication: IRR prophylaxis and steroid component of regimen

pre_medications.timing: 1-3 hours prior to daratumumab injection. Maximum 4000mg/24h
1-3 hours prior to daratumumab – first four doses only

take_home_medicines.days: Days 1-21 NOCTE
Non-daratumumab steroid days as per cycle (cycle 1: days 2,8,9,15,16,22,23; cycle 3-6: days 2,15,16; cycle 7+: day 2)
Once daily for 7 days – first cycle only
Twice daily for 28 days
Once daily for 84 days (12 weeks, cycle 1 only)
Once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday for 28 days
Once daily if recurrent oral candidiasis
On days of daratumumab administration, 1-3 hours prior
take_home_medicines.indication: IRR prophylaxis – supply 1 x OP to cover all cycles

take_home_medicines.days: Daily
take_home_medicines.indication: DVT prophylaxis per local formulary – options: dalteparin 5000 units SC OD, enoxaparin 40mg SC OD, heparin 5000 units SC BD, apixaban 2.5mg twice daily (unlicensed), aspirin 75mg OD (no or one risk factor)

dose_modifications.condition: Fall to less than 1.0x10⁹/L – first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt lenalidomide and monitor FBC weekly. Consider G-CSF. If returns to 1.0 with no other observed toxicity: resume at 25mg daily. If returns to 1.0 with dose-dependent haematological toxicities: resume at next lower dose
Interrupt lenalidomide, monitor FBC weekly. Consider G-CSF. Resume at next lower dose when ≥1.0

dose_modifications.condition: Fall to less than 30x10⁹/L – first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt lenalidomide, monitor FBC weekly. Consider transfusion. Resume at next lower dose when ≥30x10⁹/L
Interrupt lenalidomide, monitor FBC weekly. Consider transfusion. Resume at next lower dose when ≥30x10⁹/L
Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L resume daratumumab. No dose reductions of daratumumab recommended
Interrupt daratumumab and monitor FBC weekly. Once platelets ≥50x10⁹/L resume daratumumab
Reduce to 10mg daily. May escalate to 15mg after 2 cycles if not responding to treatment and tolerates the dose

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine clearance less than 30 ml/min (no dialysis)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 7.5mg daily or 15mg every other day

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine clearance less than 30 ml/min with dialysis
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 5mg daily. Administer after dialysis on dialysis days
No formal studies conducted; no specific dose recommendations
No formal studies conducted. Based on population PK analysis no dosage adjustments necessary
Stop treatment. Restart at next lower dose level when toxicity resolves to grade 2 or lower
Withhold until symptoms resolve to grade 1 or below. May be cautiously restarted at 15mg daily. Grade 3 or above: discontinue
Interrupt or discontinue lenalidomide. Must discontinue for angioedema, anaphylaxis, SJS, TEN or DRESS
Suspend dosing until PML excluded. If confirmed, permanently discontinue
Discontinue treatment. Once stabilised on anticoagulation and complications managed, lenalidomide may be restarted at original dose dependent on benefit/risk assessment

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle of daratumumab, neutrophil count should be ≥1x10⁹/L and platelet count ≥50x10⁹/L unless low counts due to bone marrow infiltration. For lenalidomide dose reduction steps: starting 25mg, level -1 20mg, level -2 15mg, level -3 10mg, level -4 5mg, level -5 2.5mg

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
HBV testing before initiating daratumumab; HCV, CMV and HIV testing if clinically appropriate
Hepatitis B reactivation monitoring during treatment and for at least 6 months after completion
Red cell phenotyping before commencing daratumumab
Pregnancy testing in women of childbearing potential: negative test within 3 days of starting lenalidomide; repeat monthly (every 2 weeks if irregular cycles); final test 4 weeks after last dose
Regular blood glucose monitoring
Monitoring for signs of thromboembolism (shortness of breath, chest pain, swelling of limb)
Neurological monitoring for PML symptoms
special_warnings: Lenalidomide is highly teratogenic – Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory; barrier contraception mandatory for men
DVT prophylaxis mandatory per local formulary
Daratumumab (SC) causes IRRs in ~11% of patients; most Grade 1-2 with first injection. Pre-medicate with antihistamine, antipyretic and corticosteroid 1-3 hours before every injection
Daratumumab binds to CD38 on red cells causing positive Coombs test – inform blood transfusion laboratory; patients must carry ID card for 6 months after stopping
Daratumumab may cause false positive SPE/IFE assays in IgG kappa myeloma patients
Hepatitis B reactivation reported with daratumumab and lenalidomide combination – screen all patients before treatment
BLUETEQ approval required before treatment initiation
Regimen based on MAIA trial (SC daratumumab; two divided dexamethasone doses). Dexamethasone dose reduced to 10mg for patients >75 years or BMI <18.5
Levofloxacin 500mg OD for 12 weeks (cycle 1 only) as antibacterial prophylaxis

ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY — NPSA/2008/RRR001: Patient must be counselled and provided written information before starting. Prescriptions must be written on an approved oral chemotherapy prescription form. Dispensing must only be by a trained pharmacist. Patient must be provided with a chemotherapy alert card. Do not supply more than one cycle at a time without clinical review.

drugs.notes: Cycles 1-2: days 1,2,8,9,15,16,22,23. Cycles 3-6: days 1,2,15,16. Cycles 7+: days 1,2. indication: Daratumumab in combination with bortezomib, dexamethasone and thalidomide is recommended for use within the Cancer Drugs Fund as an option for treating newly diagnosed multiple myeloma patients who are eligible for transplant. Based on CASSIOPEIA trial (SC instead of IV daratumumab; weekly bortezomib instead of twice-weekly; dexamethasone days 22-23 on cycles 3-6). Cycles 1-4 prior to autologous stem cell transplant; cycles 5-6 post transplant.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands (2.5). VZV prophylaxis mandatory. SC administration preferred.

SC injection into abdomen approximately 7.5cm to right or left of navel over 3-5 minutes. Rotate injection sites. Pre-medications required 1-3 hours before every injection. First dose: observe for 4 hours. If no IRR following first dose, no cannulation or post-injection observations needed for subsequent doses. Post-injection corticosteroids to reduce delayed IRR risk – if no major IRRs after first 3 injections, post-injection corticosteroids (excluding background regimen corticosteroids) may be discontinued. If no major IRRs after first 4 injections, inhaled post-injection medicinal products may be discontinued.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Cycles 1-2: days 1,2,8,9,15,16,22,23. Cycles 3-6: days 1,2,8,9,15,16,22,23. On daratumumab days, pre-med dexamethasone is sufficient; on non-daratumumab days 20mg to be administered
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Reduce to 10mg orally (or IV dose equivalent) for patients over 75 years. On daratumumab days, pre-med dexamethasone dose is sufficient. Available as 2mg and 500microgram tablets and 3.3mg in 1ml injection (equivalent to 4mg orally).

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Daily (start at 50mg and increase to 100mg as tolerated)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take at night to avoid daytime drowsiness. Start at 50mg and escalate to 100mg if appropriate. Only available as 50mg capsules – dose modifications in multiples of 50mg. Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory. Prescriptions must be accompanied by Prescription Authorisation Form.

dose_modifications.parameter: Bortezomib – Neuropathy Grade 1 (no pain/loss of function)
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 1 neuropathy without pain or loss of function
dose_modifications.notes: Continue full dose bortezomib

dose_modifications.parameter: Bortezomib – Neuropathy Grade 1 with pain or Grade 2
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 1 with pain or grade 2 neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce bortezomib to 1mg/m²

dose_modifications.parameter: Bortezomib – Neuropathy Grade 2 with pain or Grade 3
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 with pain or grade 3 neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue until resolved to grade 1 or less; reinitiate at 0.7mg/m²

dose_modifications.parameter: Bortezomib – Neuropathy Grade 4 or severe autonomic
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 or severe autonomic neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue bortezomib permanently
Discontinue until resolved to grade 1 or below. First occurrence: reinitiate at 1mg/m². Second occurrence: reduce to 0.7mg/m². If not resolved at lowest dose, consider discontinuation
May continue. If symptoms worsen, consider dose reduction or interruption. Reintroduce at reduced dose if symptoms resolve
Suspend or reduce dose by at least 50%. Reintroduce at reduced dose if symptoms resolve to grade 1 or below

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above or toxicity not resolving despite dose reduction
dose_modifications.notes: Stop thalidomide treatment

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above other toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Stop until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. Cautious reintroduction at 50mg with dose escalation if tolerated
Full anticoagulation required. Thalidomide may be stopped but can be re-introduced initially at 50mg daily with escalation at subsequent cycles to 100mg assuming good anticoagulant control

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: To initiate a new cycle, neutrophil count ≥1x10⁹/L and platelet count ≥50x10⁹/L unless low counts due to bone marrow infiltration

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, Ca²⁺ and LFTs prior to day 1 of each cycle
Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle
HBV, HCV, CMV and HIV testing before initiating treatment
Red cell phenotyping before commencing daratumumab
Pregnancy testing: negative test within 3 days prior to starting thalidomide; repeat every 4 weeks (every 2 weeks if irregular cycles); final test 4 weeks after last dose
Regular blood glucose monitoring
Peripheral neuropathy monitoring – regular neurological assessment
VTE monitoring
special_warnings: Thalidomide is highly teratogenic – Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory

DVT/PE prophylaxis mandatory – all patients initially prescribed LMWH at prophylactic dose; therapeutic warfarin or aspirin 75mg alternatives. Thromboprophylaxis recommended for at least first 5 months
Bortezomib VZV prophylaxis mandatory (aciclovir 400mg twice daily)
Daratumumab SC IRRs in ~11% – premedication mandatory; first dose in setting with resuscitation facilities; observe for 4 hours post first dose
Daratumumab binds to CD38 on RBCs – inform blood transfusion; patients must carry ID card for 6 months
Peripheral neuropathy monitoring essential for both bortezomib and thalidomide
Cycles 1-4 pre-transplant; cycles 5-6 post-transplant (NICE stipulation)
Weekly bortezomib schedule (modified from CASSIOPEIA twice-weekly to reduce neurotoxicity)
Hepatitis B reactivation reported with daratumumab – screen all patients before treatment

ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY — NPSA/2008/RRR001: Patient must be counselled and provided written information before starting. Prescriptions must be written on an approved oral chemotherapy prescription form. Dispensing must only be by a trained pharmacist. Patient must be provided with a chemotherapy alert card. Do not supply more than one cycle at a time without clinical review.

indication: Daratumumab in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone is recommended for use within the Cancer Drugs Fund as an option for treating relapsed multiple myeloma in people who have had 1 previous treatment. Twice-weekly bortezomib regimen. Note: both regimens include 32 doses of bortezomib; bortezomib continues to end of cycle 10 in weekly regimen. Cycle length is 21 days for cycles 1-8, changing to 28 days from cycle 9 onwards (see separate protocol).

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands (2.5). VZV prophylaxis mandatory. SC administration.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Cycles 1-3: weekly (days 1,8,15); Cycles 4-8: monthly (day 1). Cycle length 21 days.
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Prescribed per national dose bands (20 NS). Infusion rate: First infusion 500ml at 25ml/hr initial, increment 25ml/hr/hr, max 100ml/hr. Second infusion 500ml at 50ml/hr initial, increment 50ml/hr/hr, max 200ml/hr. Subsequent infusions 500ml at 100ml/hr initial, increment 50ml/hr/hr, max 200ml/hr. Cycles 1-3 (21-day): days 1,8,15 weekly. Cycles 4-8 (21-day): day 1 monthly. Cycle 9 onwards see separate protocol (28-day cycle). Rapid infusion protocol (90 minutes, unlicensed) available from 3rd infusion in eligible patients – 100ml over 30 min then 400ml over 60 min. Pre-medications required 1-3 hours before every daratumumab infusion.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Cycles 1-3: days 1,2,4,5,8,9,11,12 (20mg IV bolus on infusion days, oral days 2,4,5,9,11,12). Cycles 4-8: days 1,2,4,5,8,9,11,12
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: 20mg IV bolus on daratumumab infusion days (as pre-medication). Can be given as 20mg orally from second infusion onwards. Cycles 4-8 day 1: 20mg oral/IV on daratumumab day. Oral days 2, 4, 5, 8, 9, 11, 12: 20mg daily. Dexamethasone 4mg given on days 16 and 17 (post-infusion to reduce delayed IRR risk, cycles 1-3 only). Reduce to 10mg IV/oral in patients over 75 years. Day after bortezomib alone (days 5, 9, 12 from cycle 4 onwards) may be reduced for elderly or intolerant patients.
